name: HubSpot
gate_reason: Free tier + free dev test accounts

name: Twenty
gate_reason: Fully open source, self-host or free cloud tier
blocker: Self-hosting adds ops overhead

name: Podio
gate_reason: Free dev account for API keys
blocker: Product is in maintenance mode, sparse updates

name: Close
gate_reason: Free trial gives API key immediately

name: Copper
gate_reason: Basic API key self-serve; full OAuth app publishing needs partner review
blocker: Marketplace listing needs Copper review

name: DealCloud
gate_reason: API key must be enabled by a site admin/CSM per user group
blocker: Needs an existing paying client account + admin to flip on API access

name: Zendesk
gate_reason: Free trial account gives full API access

name: Pylon
auth: Bearer API token; MCP uses OAuth2 (AuthKit)

name: Plain
gate_reason: Free tier, API-first product by design

name: Gladly
gate_reason: Enterprise-only sales-led product, no self-serve signup
blocker: Requires an existing paid contract to get credentials

name: Zoho Cliq
one_liner: Team chat, part of Zoho suite

name: Pumble
gate_reason: No fully public developer REST API; mainly incoming webhooks + limited endpoints, no self-serve app platform
blocker: No documented general-purpose REST API for building an agent toolkit

name: Discord
gate_reason: Free developer portal, instant bot token

name: Telegram
auth: Bot token (BotFather) / MTProto for client API

name: WhatsApp Business
gate_reason: Free sandbox/dev tier; production sending needs Meta Business verification
blocker: Business verification + template approval for production use

name: Google Ads
gate_reason: Requires a Google Ads developer token approved by Google (basic access is easyish; standard access needs review)
blocker: Developer token approval step + review for higher access tiers

name: Meta Ads
gate_reason: App review required for most ad-management permissions beyond your own ad account
blocker: Meta App Review for production-level permissions

name: LinkedIn Ads
gate_reason: Marketing API access requires LinkedIn Partner Program application/approval
blocker: Partner approval is a hard gate, not just a form

name: GoHighLevel
gate_reason: API access requires Unlimited/SaaS Pro plan; OAuth apps need Marketplace review to go public
blocker: Paid-plan gate + marketplace review for public distribution

name: Klaviyo
gate_reason: Free tier account gives private API key

name: systeme.io
gate_reason: Free plan includes API key generation

name: Threads (Meta)
gate_reason: Self-serve for own account; broader use needs Meta app review
api_surface: REST (Threads API), narrower than Graph API
blocker: App review for anything beyond basic self-use

name: Shopify
auth: OAuth2 (public apps), API access token (custom/private apps)

name: WooCommerce
auth: API key (Consumer key/secret, OAuth1.0a for HTTP)
gate_reason: Self-hosted, generate keys yourself, fully free

name: Salesforce Commerce Cloud
gate_reason: Requires an existing SFCC instance/sandbox, typically partner/client provisioned
blocker: Needs provisioned sandbox from Salesforce/partner

name: Magento (Adobe Commerce)
gate_reason: Open-source edition self-serve/self-host; Adobe Commerce (paid) is sales-gated
blocker: Enterprise tier gated; open-source tier self-serve

name: Squarespace
gate_reason: Commerce APIs need a Business/Commerce plan site
blocker: Requires paid Commerce-tier site to unlock API keys

name: Amazon Selling Partner
gate_reason: Requires an active, approved Amazon Seller/Vendor account and app registration/review for many roles
blocker: Seller Central account approval + role-based application review

name: fanbasis
gate_reason: Free account, generate key in Account > API Keys

name: DataForSEO
gate_reason: Free trial credit on signup, instant

name: SE Ranking
gate_reason: API is add-on / higher-tier plan dependent
blocker: API access tied to paid plan tier/add-on

name: Ahrefs
gate_reason: API is a separate paid add-on on top of a subscription
blocker: Requires paid API subscription add-on, no free key

name: Sherlock
one_liner: OSINT username-search tool (CLI, not a hosted API)
gate_reason: Fully open-source, run locally, no account needed
api_surface: No API — it's a Python CLI script, not a service
blocker: Not a hosted service/API at all; would need to be wrapped as a script-runner tool, raising ToS/ethics questions per-target-site

name: Waterfall.io
one_liner: B2B contact/company data enrichment API (aggregates 30+ vendors)
gate_reason: Every user gets an API key immediately after signup; usage-based pricing, no long-term contract needed
api_surface: REST, focused (prospector, contact/phone/company enrichment, email verification)

name: Clay
gate_reason: Free tier + API key available on paid-ish/pro plans

name: Neo4j
api_surface: Bolt protocol + REST (Aura mgmt) + GraphQL (via plugin)
blocker: Query surface is Cypher over Bolt, not a typical REST CRUD API — needs a driver, not just HTTP calls

name: MongoDB Atlas
gate_reason: Free M0 tier, instant API key

name: Plaid
blocker: Production access needs Plaid review, but Sandbox is fully self-serve for building/testing

name: Binance
gate_reason: Free account, instant API key generation
blocker: Some endpoints geo-restricted

name: Paygent Connect
gate_reason: Appears to be provisioned per-merchant via a payment processor/reseller relationship, not a public self-serve dev portal
blocker: No public, self-serve developer docs found; likely requires a merchant account setup with the processor

name: iPayX
one_liner: Payment gateway / fintech infra (marketed toward AI agents)
gate_reason: Marketing suggests developer-first access but docs are thin/early-stage; likely needs signup approval typical of payment processors
blocker: Payment-processor-style KYC/approval likely required before live keys

name: Brex
gate_reason: Requires an existing funded Brex business account
blocker: Needs an actual approved Brex account (business banking underwriting)

name: Ramp
gate_reason: Requires an existing approved Ramp customer account
blocker: Needs an approved Ramp business account

name: PitchBook
gate_reason: Standalone paid contract required; no public docs, no sandbox without an account manager
blocker: Requires a signed standalone contract + account-manager-issued key; no public docs or self-serve sandbox

name: NotebookLM
gate_reason: Consumer NotebookLM has no public API; programmatic access is only via Gemini Enterprise API for enterprise customers
api_surface: REST (via Gemini Enterprise), not the consumer product itself
blocker: Consumer product has no API; enterprise tier requires a Google Cloud/Gemini Enterprise contract

name: Otter AI
auth: OAuth2 (official MCP); public REST API is Enterprise-gated beta
gate_reason: MCP server is OAuth self-serve-ish (workspace admin may need to enable); the underlying public REST API requires an Enterprise plan/account-manager
api_surface: MCP (official, OAuth) is broader than the REST API, which is beta/Enterprise-only
blocker: Full REST API access needs Enterprise; MCP is the more accessible path today

name: Fathom
auth: API key (Team/Enterprise), Zapier/webhooks for lower tiers
gate_reason: Native REST API historically limited to Team/Enterprise plans; broader access via Zapier
blocker: Direct API access gated behind higher-tier plans

name: Consensus
auth: OAuth2 (official MCP server); documented REST API for higher-volume/enterprise use
gate_reason: MCP server is self-serve (free account + OAuth); the high-volume REST 'quick search' API is 'by application only' with per-call pricing
blocker: Full-volume REST API needs an application/quote for enterprise pricing

name: Devin
gate_reason: API/MCP access tied to a paid Devin plan (no free self-serve tier for the agent itself)
blocker: Requires a paid Devin subscription to get credentials

name: higgsfield
gate_reason: CLI/API access appears tied to paid credits/plan; not fully documented as an open self-serve REST API
blocker: Thin public docs; CLI-first product, not a conventional REST API

name: Mermaid CLI
gate_reason: Fully open-source npm package, no account needed
api_surface: No hosted API — local CLI/Node library only
verdict: ready today (as a local tool call, not a hosted API)
blocker: Not a hosted service, so 'toolkit' would mean shelling out to a local binary, not calling a remote API

name: YouTube Transcript
gate_reason: Free tier/trial on signup (unofficial, third-party service)
blocker: Not an official YouTube product — depends on an unofficial third party, so reliability/ToS risk is the real blocker

name: Grain
gate_reason: Full API access historically tied to Business/Enterprise plan
blocker: Direct API access gated behind higher-tier plan
